commit.message:
Optimize throughput: 3.6-5.4x speedup via selective models and batched inference (#3)

* Optimize face recognition throughput with model-selective pipelines and batched inference

Bypass InsightFace's app.get() which runs all 5 models per face regardless of endpoint.
Each endpoint now runs only the models it needs: detect() skips 4 models, embed() skips
landmarks and genderage, analyze() skips landmarks. Recognition is batched via get_feat()
across all faces within and across images. Batch endpoints use cross-image batching with
a single semaphore acquisition.

RTX 4090 results: detect 78ms→14.5ms (5.4x), embed 78ms→21.5ms (3.6x),
embed_batch(8) 616ms→157ms (3.9x). Embeddings verified identical (cosine sim 1.0).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix import sorting in test_face_provider.py

* Remove comments and docstrings from source files

commit.message:
perf(narrative): rewrite _update_graph_connections to eliminate O(n²) timeout

Old approach: fetch ALL active storylines (~3163) per updated storyline,
iterate in Python, run per-pair UPSERT+DELETE → ~95K DB queries → 15+ min → timeout.

New approach:
1. Pre-filter at DB level with EXISTS+unnest+LOWER+ANY: only storylines that
   share ≥1 entity are returned (~10-50 instead of ~3163, no info loss)
2. DELETE all outgoing edges in one statement (source_story_id = %s)
3. Bulk-insert valid edges with execute_values (one INSERT for all valid edges)

Expected: narrative_processing step goes from >1800s to <5 min.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Rules of Hooks violation on playbook page — crash when Supabase down

The tier gate early return (line 222) was firing BEFORE the two useEffect
calls on lines 249 and 335. When Supabase is unreachable, plan-tier API
returns "free", tier gate fires, React sees fewer hooks than previous
render → error boundary ("Playbook couldn't load").

Fix: move tier gate conditional return to AFTER all useEffect/hook calls.
All hooks now execute unconditionally every render. Tier gate logic is
identical — just positioned correctly per React Rules of Hooks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(parser): reject inputs with too many unmatched open brackets

The fuzzer found a 193-byte input with 10 unmatched `[` brackets that
caused pest to spend >30s in exponential backtracking trying every
combination of array_literal, index_access, and slice_access rules.

Add a pre-parse check that counts unmatched open brackets per type and
rejects inputs exceeding 6 (generous for real VPL; unmatched brackets
are always parse errors anyway). This catches the pathological case in
O(n) before pest begins parsing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Migrate to CatBoost as primary GBM and Polars as data layer

Replace LightGBM with CatBoost throughout: CatBoost handles categorical
features natively without label encoding and its SHAP implementation works
correctly with shap's TreeExplainer for log-link objectives.

Replace pandas with Polars as the primary data structure in all library
internals and the public API. Pandas is retained as a bridge dependency
only — shap's TreeExplainer requires a pandas DataFrame for column name
handling, so _to_pandas() is called in fit() and validate() only.

Changes:
- pyproject.toml: polars replaces pandas in core deps; catboost replaces
  lightgbm in [ml]/[all]/[dev] extras; lightgbm moved to its own optional
  extra; version bumped to 0.2.0
- _aggregation.py: rewritten in Polars using group_by().agg() with explicit
  weighted mean/variance expressions
- _normalisation.py: rewritten in Polars using with_columns() expressions
- _validation.py: check_sparse_levels accepts pl.DataFrame
- _core.py: SHAPRelativities accepts pl.DataFrame or pd.DataFrame as X;
  extract_relativities() returns pl.DataFrame; all internal accumulation
  uses pl.concat; _to_polars()/_to_pandas() bridge helpers added
- _plotting.py: operates on pl.DataFrame, uses .to_numpy() for arrays
- datasets/motor.py: load_motor() returns pl.DataFrame; data generation
  builds arrays with numpy and assembles Polars DataFrame directly
- tests: all updated for Polars API; test_shap_relativities.py uses
  CatBoost with a Pool bridge; test_accepts_pandas_input added

All 73 tests pass on Databricks serverless (catboost 1.2+, polars 0.20+,
shap 0.42+, numpy<2.0).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(mcp): atomic state file writes to prevent JSON corruption (#85)

## Summary

- `saveJson` now writes to `filePath + '.tmp'` then calls `renameSync`
to atomically replace the target — POSIX `rename()` is always either old
or new content, never partial
- On error, attempts to clean up the `.tmp` file via `unlinkSync`
- Fixes `SyntaxError: Unexpected non-whitespace character after JSON` in
`refresh-tokens.json` caused by Docker restarts mid-write
- Bumps MCP version to 0.1.41

## Test plan

- [ ] All 324 tests pass (`npm test`)
- [ ] Prettier clean (`npm run format`)
- [ ] Deploy to prod and confirm no corrupt-JSON warn in logs on restart

---------

Add pipeline health monitoring to dashboard

When the self-hosted runner goes offline, the dashboard now detects
and warns users instead of silently serving stale data:

- Dashboard: staleness banner appears when data is >3 hours old,
  shows "pipeline may have stopped" or "runner is offline" with
  runner status from system-status.json
- Workflow: runner-health.yml runs every 4h on GitHub-hosted runner
  (ubuntu-latest), checks self-hosted runner status via API, writes
  docs/data/system-status.json with runner health + cancelled run count
- .gitignore: whitelist system-status.json

This closes the "runner down" detection gap — the only monitoring
blind spot that required external infrastructure.

commit.message:
feat: add chat mute system (#499)

## Summary

- Adds `chatMutes?: PlayerBan[]` field to `PlayerModel` (same shape as
existing `bans`)
- Implements `addChatMute` and `revokeChatMute` business logic with
typed event emission
- Adds `hasActiveChatMute()` pure helper with full unit test coverage
- Enforces mute check in `chat/send.ts` — muted players get a 403 error
- Admin UI: new **Chat mutes** tab on the player edit page (list +
inline revoke + add form)
- Chat input is disabled with placeholder "you are currently muted" for
muted players

## Test plan

- [ ] All unit tests pass (`pnpm test`)
- [ ] `pnpm lint` and `pnpm build` pass
- [ ] As admin, navigate to `/players/:steamId/edit/chat-mutes` and add
a mute
- [ ] As muted player, verify the chat input is disabled with correct
placeholder
- [ ] Verify muted player cannot post messages (403 on form submit)
- [ ] As admin, revoke a mute inline — verify it updates in place
- [ ] Verify expired mutes are shown as inactive and cannot be revoked
again

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
